column3: The event in which a material sample was physically removed from the painting.
Document Note: Sample Taking Model Overview

column1: Document Note: This model captures metadata specific to an individual sample taking event within a broader Sampling Activity. Many fields here are optional because values defined at the Sampling Activity level -- such as date, method, location, and responsible actor -- apply to all sample takings within that activity and do not need to be repeated unless they differ for this specific event. Fields that are always recorded at this level include the link to the sample produced, the sample site, the heritage object, and the system identifier.

column3: E22 is a duplicate to the one defined at the parent activity but a key property is required here for the CRM Sci linking the S2, via an O3 to the E22.

A curated physical artefact that contains or carries the sampled matter (S13). Serves as the object-management layer: it holds identifiers, condition records, custody, storage, and images. P46 is used as a pragmatic contains/carries proxy for collection management purposes.

The removed matter treated as an amount of matter (CRMsci S13) whose identity is grounded in material continuity and representativity. Serves as the scientific layer: it is the subject of measurement, observation, modification, and splitting events.
Primarily related to authored notes, reports and publications, however there is a whole range of relevant documents that can reference or be linked to this event.
The defined process or protocol followed during the sampling or analysis activity (if different from method indicated in sampling activity).

Name of person (or institution) who took the sample (if different from the person/institution defined at sampling activity level). When person is unknown, ‘unknown person’ or the institution (which is usually known) should be recorded.

column1: //links E39: Sample taken by --> person, organisation [confirmed]

column3: The physical place where the sampling event occurred, typically within an organisation or laboratory.
The specific point or small area on the heritage object where the sample was taken.
An optional public unique identifier (PID) for the sample taking event. Any of the IDs defined could be flagged as a preferred ID within a documentation system.
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.
Automatic: Preferred Painting ID - Sample Label - Sampling DATE - TBC
Sample-specific reasons (different to the general reason indicated at the Sampling Activity Level).

A brief factual description of the specific sampling event, focusing on any notable aspects of how the sample was removed or the nature of the material extracted. Use this to record observations, exceptions, or deviations from standard procedures, rather than to restate the sampling method, motivation, or site details.
The optional defined period of the specific sample taking event – if different from the parent sampling activity.

The material or material class that motivated the sampling, such as a varnish layer, paint component, or specific pigment. This field records the intended material focus of sampling and does not imply that the material was identified or confirmed analytically.
The current condition, treatment state, or lifecycle position of the sample site: conservation history, handling, availability, or degree of alteration over time.
